name: Paul & Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans
eligibility: New Americans (immigrants and children of immigrants) age 32 or younger pursuing graduate degrees
why: $90,000 over two years for graduate school. Join a network of 835+ Fellows across 103 countries making significant contributions to US society, culture and academia.

name: Thiel Fellowship
eligibility: Young people age 22 or younger building new ventures
why: $200,000 over two years to skip college and build something new. Access to Peter Thiel's network of founders, investors, and scientists.

name: IIM Ahmedabad AI Summer Residency 2026
eligibility: STEM undergraduates comfortable building end-to-end prototypes; deadline March 31
why: Free 4-6 week residency with housing, cloud credits, and mentorship to build production-ready AI products. Move beyond hackathon demos to real-world applications.

name: Breakthrough Junior Challenge 2026
eligibility: Students ages 13–18 worldwide; submit a 2-minute video explaining a concept in physics, life sciences, or mathematics
why: $250,000 post-secondary scholarship, a $50k teacher prize, and a $100k school lab for the best 2-minute science video. No lab or team required; open now through September 15.

name: MCW Global Young Leaders Fellowship 2026
organizer: MCW Global (Miracle Corners of the World)
eligibility: Young people worldwide ages 18-26; fluent English required
why: Fully funded year-long leadership program with 10-day in-person training, alumni mentorship, and $15k+ project funding. Past graduates lead major NGOs and social enterprises.

name: JAMHacks 10
eligibility: High school students, free attendance with accommodations
why: Canada's largest high school hackathon celebrating 10 years. 36 hours of building with free meals, accommodations, mentorship, and prizes. Strong hacker density makes co-founder connections likely.

name: ETHGlobal New York 2026
eligibility: Open to all builders; 800+ attendees expected
why: Premier Web3 hackathon with $150k+ prize pool. 3 days of building with free food, technical mentorship, and exclusive workshops. Refundable ETH stake required to ensure commitment.

name: HackPrix Season 3
eligibility: University students interested in technology and innovation
why: 36-hour MLH-backed hackathon with 700+ students and $3000+ prize pool. Build for social good with mentor support, workshops, and networking at one of India's premier student tech events.

name: USAII Global AI Hackathon 2026
eligibility: High school (grades 9-12), undergraduate, graduate, and doctoral students worldwide
why: Premier global AI competition with $15k+ prize pool. Students build real-world AI solutions with mentor support. Award winners receive AI certification scholarships.

name: YC Summer Fellows Grants
eligibility: Undergraduate CS or engineering students worldwide; no company required
why: $20k cash + $90k in compute credits to build a self-directed AI project in SF. YC picks 20–50 students; no company or team required.

name: YC AI Startup School
eligibility: CS undergrads, masters, and PhD students
why: Free, in-person, highly selective. Speakers include Sam Altman, Andrej Karpathy, and Andrew Ng — for CS students who want to build AI companies, this is the room.

name: AI Hackathon 2026
eligibility: Undergrad/grad students from any institution, recent grads (Fall 2024+); apply by May 17
why: $100,000 in prizes across four specialized tracks. 1,300+ hackers, free meals and accommodations. One of the largest student AI hackathons in the US with strong industry connections.

name: Apple AIML Residency 2026
eligibility: Master's/PhD graduates, software engineers, academics seeking industry experience
why: One-year paid residency at Apple working on ML products that impact millions. Residents publish at premier venues like NeurIPS while gaining direct Apple mentor access.

name: Anthropic AI Safety Fellows
eligibility: Engineers and researchers with strong Python background; must have right to work in US, UK, or Canada
why: $3,850/week stipend and $15k/month in compute for full-time AI safety research. 80%+ of past fellows published a paper; 40%+ received a full-time Anthropic offer.

name: ETHGlobal Lisbon 2026
eligibility: Open to all builders worldwide
why: Major Ethereum hackathon with substantial prize pool, free meals, and mentorship from top blockchain protocols. Small refundable ETH stake required; alternatives available for financial constraints.

name: IAPS AI Policy Fellowship
organizer: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy
eligibility: Undergrads through professionals worldwide; DC track requires US work authorization
why: $15–22k stipend, two-week DC residency, direct mentorship from leading AI governance experts. Past fellows joined CSET, GovAI, and major tech policy shops.

name: Richard Tapia Celebration of Diversity in Computing
eligibility: Students from underrepresented groups in computing; scholarships available
why: Full scholarships covering travel, hotel, and registration. Career fair access to Google, Microsoft, and Meta engineers that is difficult to replicate any other way.

name: HackMIT 2026
eligibility: Undergraduate and graduate students from any university worldwide; applications open in August
why: MIT's flagship 24-hour hackathon with 1,000+ college hackers. Sponsor challenges include cash prizes and direct internship and full-time interview pipelines at top tech companies.

name: MHacks 2026
eligibility: Students currently enrolled in or recently left any high school, college, or university worldwide
why: $30,000+ in prizes at one of the world's largest collegiate hackathons. 1,000+ hackers from 100+ universities over 36 hours with strong alumni-to-company placement track record.

name: ETHGlobal Tokyo 2026
eligibility: Open to all builders worldwide
why: Elite Web3 hackathon in Asia's tech capital. Large prize pool, beginner-friendly mentorship, and access to leading blockchain innovators. Refundable ETH stake ensures participant commitment.

name: Grace Hopper Celebration 2026
eligibility: Students and professionals who identify as women or non-binary in tech; scholarships available
why: The world's largest gathering of women in tech (~30,000 attendees). AnitaB.org scholarships cover full attendance. Career fair is a direct path to top company offers.
